questions.stem_en: A car travels at 60 km/h for 3 hours. What distance does it cover?
questions.stem_hi: एक car 60 km/h की चाल से 3 घंटे चलती है। दूरी कितनी होगी?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Divided instead of multiplied: 60/3 = 20. Formula is distance = speed × time, not speed ÷ time.
options.trap_reason_hi: Multiply की जगह divide किया: 60/3 = 20। Formula: distance = speed × time।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Added speed and time treating them as like quantities: 60 + 3 = 63. Speed and time are different dimensions.
options.trap_reason_hi: Speed और time को जोड़ दिया: 60 + 3 = 63। ये अलग dimensions हैं।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used 4 hours instead of 3: 60 × 4 = 240. Misread the duration.

questions.explanation_method_en: Distance = Speed × Time = 60 × 3 = 180 km.
questions.explanation_concept_en: The three quantities are linked by D = S × T. Given any two, the third follows. Keep units consistent — here km/h × h = km. Mixing km/h with minutes silently produces unit errors; convert first if needed.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Distance = Speed × Time = 60 × 3 = 180 km।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: तीनों राशियाँ D = S × T से जुड़ी हैं। दो दी हों तो तीसरी निकलती है। Units consistent रखें: km/h × h = km। km/h को minutes के साथ mix करने पर silent unit error; पहले convert करें।

questions.stem_en: A cyclist covers 45 km in 3 hours. Find her average speed.
questions.stem_hi: एक cyclist 3 घंटे में 45 km की दूरी तय करती है। उसकी average speed क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Multiplied distance by time: 45 × 3 = 135. Speed = distance/time, not distance × time.
options.trap_reason_hi: Distance × time कर दिया: 45 × 3 = 135। Speed = distance/time होता है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracted time from distance: 45 − 3 = 42. Wrong operation — distance and time aren't subtractable quantities.
options.trap_reason_hi: Distance में से time घटाया: 45 − 3 = 42। Distance और time घटाए नहीं जाते।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Inverted the formula: computed time/distance = 3/45 = 1/15. Speed is the direct ratio, not its reciprocal.
options.trap_reason_hi: Formula उल्टी लगाई: time/distance = 3/45 = 1/15 निकाला। Speed direct ratio है, उल्टी नहीं।

questions.explanation_method_en: Speed = Distance / Time = 45/3 = 15 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Average speed is total distance divided by total time. For a single leg at constant pace, it equals the instantaneous speed. For multi-leg journeys at different paces, average ≠ (sum of speeds)/n — it's still total distance / total time.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Speed = Distance / Time = 45/3 = 15 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Average speed = total distance / total time। एक ही pace वाली यात्रा में यह instantaneous speed के बराबर होती है। Multi-leg यात्रा में average ≠ (speeds का sum)/n — total distance / total time ही है।

questions.stem_en: Convert 54 km/h to m/s.
questions.stem_hi: 54 km/h को m/s में बदलिए।

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Multiplied by 3.6 instead of dividing: 54 × 3.6 = 194.4. The factor 18/5 (= 3.6) goes m/s → km/h; the inverse is 5/18.
options.trap_reason_hi: 3.6 से भाग देने की जगह multiply किया: 54 × 3.6 = 194.4। 3.6 m/s → km/h के लिए है, उल्टी दिशा में 5/18 लगेगा।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Divided by 6 (approximated the factor as 6): 54/6 = 9. Correct conversion factor is 18/5 = 3.6.
options.trap_reason_hi: 6 से भाग दिया (factor को 6 मान लिया): 54/6 = 9। सही factor 18/5 = 3.6।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Converted km → m but skipped the h → s step: 54 × 1000 / 360 = 150 (divided by 360 instead of 3600).

questions.explanation_method_en: Multiply by 5/18 to go km/h → m/s: 54 × 5/18 = 270/18 = 15 m/s.
questions.explanation_concept_en: 1 km = 1000 m and 1 h = 3600 s, so 1 km/h = 1000/3600 = 5/18 m/s. Memorize this fraction and its reciprocal 18/5 for rapid two-way conversion in the exam. Errors come from dropping either the distance or the time part of the conversion.
questions.explanation_concept_hi: 1 km = 1000 m और 1 h = 3600 s, अतः 1 km/h = 5/18 m/s। यह fraction और इसका reciprocal 18/5 याद रखें — exam में जल्दी convert करने के लिए।

questions.stem_en: A train 120 m long is running at 72 km/h. How long does it take to cross a stationary pole?
questions.stem_hi: एक 120 m लंबी train 72 km/h की चाल से चल रही है। एक खड़े pole को पार करने में कितना समय लगेगा?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Skipped km/h → m/s conversion: computed 120/72 = 1.67 treating 72 as m/s.
options.trap_reason_hi: km/h → m/s conversion छोड़ दी: 120/72 = 1.67 निकाला, 72 को m/s मान लिया।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used speed 54 km/h = 15 m/s from a memorized example: 120/15 = 8. Actual speed 72 km/h = 20 m/s.
options.trap_reason_hi: Memorize किया हुआ example (54 km/h = 15 m/s) लगा दिया: 120/15 = 8। असली speed 72 km/h = 20 m/s।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Converted 72 km/h to 20 m/s correctly but divided 120 by 60 instead of 20 (slipped a decimal): 120/60 = 2.

questions.explanation_method_en: Speed = 72 × 5/18 = 20 m/s. Distance = length of train = 120 m (pole is a point). Time = 120/20 = 6 s.
questions.explanation_concept_en: When a train crosses a stationary point (pole, person, signal), the distance covered equals the train's own length — the front reaches the point, then the whole train must pass. Convert speed to m/s so the units align with the length in metres.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Speed = 72 × 5/18 = 20 m/s। Distance = train की length = 120 m (pole point है)। Time = 120/20 = 6 sec।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Stationary point (pole, person, signal) को cross करते समय covered distance = train की length। Speed को m/s में बदलें ताकि length (m में) के साथ units match हों।

questions.stem_en: A bus travels from city A to city B at 40 km/h and returns at 60 km/h by the same route. Find the average speed for the whole journey.
questions.stem_hi: एक bus city A से B तक 40 km/h से और वापस same route पर 60 km/h से लौटती है। पूरी यात्रा की average speed क्या है?

options.label: A
options.trap_reason_en: Arithmetic mean: (40 + 60)/2 = 50. Arithmetic mean of speeds is only correct when equal TIME is spent at each speed, not equal DISTANCE.
options.trap_reason_hi: Arithmetic mean: (40 + 60)/2 = 50। यह तभी सही है जब हर speed पर equal TIME हो, equal DISTANCE नहीं।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Picked a value midway but biased high — used a weighted average with wrong weights (bias towards 60).
options.trap_reason_hi: Midway value लगाई पर 60 की ओर झुकी — wrong weights वाला weighted average।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used (40 × 60)/(40 + 60) without the × 2: 2400/100 = 24. Harmonic mean formula requires the 2× in the numerator for equal distances.
options.trap_reason_hi: (40 × 60)/(40 + 60) में × 2 भूल गए: 2400/100 = 24। Harmonic mean formula में numerator 2× होता है (equal distances के लिए)।

questions.explanation_method_en: Equal distance each way: avg speed = 2ab/(a+b) = 2 × 40 × 60 / 100 = 4800/100 = 48 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: When equal DISTANCES are travelled at different speeds, the time spent at the slower speed is more, dragging the average below the arithmetic mean. The formula 2ab/(a+b) is the harmonic mean of the two speeds — derived from total distance / total time where each leg has equal distance d: total time = d/a + d/b, total distance = 2d, ratio = 2ab/(a+b).
questions.explanation_method_hi: Equal distance each way: avg speed = 2ab/(a+b) = 2 × 40 × 60 / 100 = 48 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Equal DISTANCES पर अलग speeds से चलने पर slower speed पर ज़्यादा time लगता है — average arithmetic mean से कम आता है। Formula 2ab/(a+b) दोनों speeds का harmonic mean है: total time = d/a + d/b, total distance = 2d, ratio = 2ab/(a+b)।

questions.stem_en: A man walks at 5 km/h for 2 hours and then runs at 10 km/h for 1 hour. What is his average speed for the whole journey?
questions.stem_hi: एक आदमी 5 km/h से 2 घंटे चलता है और फिर 10 km/h से 1 घंटा दौड़ता है। पूरी यात्रा की average speed क्या है?

options.label: A
options.trap_reason_en: Used arithmetic mean of the two speeds: (5 + 10)/2 = 7.5. That's correct only if equal TIME is split and weights are equal — here times are 2 h and 1 h, not equal.
options.trap_reason_hi: दोनों speeds का arithmetic mean: (5 + 10)/2 = 7.5। यह केवल equal times पर सही है, यहाँ 2 h और 1 h हैं।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Swapped the weights: used (5 × 1 + 10 × 2)/3 = 25/3 = 8.33. Man walks 2 h and runs 1 h, so weights are 2 on 5 and 1 on 10.

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Added the total distances (10 + 10 = 20) and divided by 'number of legs' = 2: 20/2 = 10. Then added 5 km/h. Muddled total distance/total time path.
options.trap_reason_hi: Total distances जोड़कर (10 + 10 = 20) legs 2 से भाग किया: 20/2 = 10, फिर 5 जोड़ दिया। Total distance / total time की सीधी राह छोड़ दी।

questions.explanation_method_en: Total distance = 5 × 2 + 10 × 1 = 10 + 10 = 20 km. Total time = 2 + 1 = 3 h. Average = 20/3 = 6.67 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Average speed is always total distance divided by total time — never a plain arithmetic mean of the speeds. When times are the weights (equal-time legs), arithmetic mean coincides; otherwise, compute distance per leg and sum, then divide by the sum of times.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Total distance = 5 × 2 + 10 × 1 = 10 + 10 = 20 km। Total time = 2 + 1 = 3 h। Average = 20/3 = 6.67 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Average speed हमेशा total distance / total time — speeds का plain arithmetic mean नहीं। Equal-time legs में arithmetic mean match कर जाता है; अन्यथा per-leg distance निकालकर जोड़ें, फिर total time से भाग दें।

questions.stem_en: Two trains of lengths 150 m and 100 m run in opposite directions on parallel tracks at 40 km/h and 50 km/h respectively. How long do they take to cross each other completely?
questions.stem_hi: 150 m और 100 m की दो trains parallel tracks पर विपरीत दिशा में क्रमशः 40 km/h और 50 km/h से चल रही हैं। एक-दूसरे को पूरी तरह cross करने में कितना समय लगेगा?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used only one train's speed 40 km/h = 11.11 m/s: 250/11.11 = 22.5 s. In opposite-direction crossing, relative speed is the SUM (90 km/h = 25 m/s), not either single speed.
options.trap_reason_hi: केवल एक train की speed 40 km/h = 11.11 m/s लगाई: 250/11.11 = 22.5 s। विपरीत दिशा में relative = SUM (90 km/h = 25 m/s)।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracted the speeds (50 − 40 = 10 km/h = 2.78 m/s) instead of adding: 250/2.78 = 90. Subtraction is for same-direction, not opposite-direction.
options.trap_reason_hi: Speeds घटा दिए (50 − 40 = 10 km/h = 2.78 m/s): 250/2.78 = 90। घटाना same-direction के लिए है।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used only one train's length (150 m) with correct relative speed 25 m/s: 150/25 = 6 s. Crossing event requires the SUM of both lengths = 250 m.
options.trap_reason_hi: केवल एक train की length (150 m) लगाई (relative speed 25 m/s सही): 150/25 = 6 s। Crossing में दोनों lengths का SUM = 250 m लगता है।

questions.explanation_method_en: Relative speed (opposite direction) = 40 + 50 = 90 km/h = 90 × 5/18 = 25 m/s. Distance to cover = 150 + 100 = 250 m. Time = 250/25 = 10 s.
questions.explanation_concept_en: When two trains cross each other, the 'distance' for the crossing event is the sum of their lengths — the rear of one must pass the rear of the other. Relative speed ADDS for opposite directions and SUBTRACTS for same direction (the observer sits on one train).
questions.explanation_method_hi: Relative speed (विपरीत दिशा) = 40 + 50 = 90 km/h = 90 × 5/18 = 25 m/s। Crossing distance = 150 + 100 = 250 m। Time = 250/25 = 10 sec।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: दो trains cross करते समय 'distance' दोनों lengths का sum होता है — एक की पिछली end दूसरी की पिछली end को pass करनी होती है। Relative speed विपरीत दिशा में जुड़ती है, same direction में घटती है।

questions.stem_en: A boat goes 24 km downstream in 3 hours and 16 km upstream in 4 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water.
questions.stem_hi: एक boat 3 घंटे में 24 km downstream और 4 घंटे में 16 km upstream तय करती है। still water में boat की speed क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Reported only the downstream speed (24/3 = 8) as the still-water speed. Still-water speed is the AVERAGE of downstream and upstream speeds.
options.trap_reason_hi: Downstream speed (24/3 = 8) को ही still-water speed मान लिया। Still-water speed downstream और upstream का AVERAGE होती है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Reported only the upstream speed (16/4 = 4). Still-water speed is midway between downstream and upstream, not either alone.
options.trap_reason_hi: केवल upstream speed (16/4 = 4) दे दी। Still-water speed downstream और upstream के बीच midway होती है।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Computed stream speed (8 − 4)/2 = 2 instead of boat speed. Stream speed = half the DIFFERENCE; boat speed = half the SUM.
options.trap_reason_hi: Stream speed (8 − 4)/2 = 2 निकाल दी, boat की speed नहीं। Stream = आधा DIFFERENCE; boat = आधा SUM।

questions.explanation_method_en: Downstream speed = 24/3 = 8 km/h. Upstream speed = 16/4 = 4 km/h. Boat in still water = (8 + 4)/2 = 6 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Let boat speed in still water = b, stream speed = s. Downstream = b + s; upstream = b − s. Adding gives 2b; subtracting gives 2s. So b = (downstream + upstream)/2 and s = (downstream − upstream)/2. Both are halves — of the sum and the difference respectively.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Downstream = 24/3 = 8 km/h। Upstream = 16/4 = 4 km/h। Still water boat = (8 + 4)/2 = 6 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Boat speed still water = b, stream speed = s मानें। Downstream = b + s, upstream = b − s। Joड़ें: 2b; घटाएँ: 2s। अतः b = (downstream + upstream)/2, s = (downstream − upstream)/2।

questions.stem_en: A person walks to his office at 5 km/h and reaches 10 minutes late. If he walks at 6 km/h, he reaches 5 minutes early. Find the distance to the office.
questions.stem_hi: एक व्यक्ति 5 km/h से office जाता है तो 10 min late पहुँचता है। 6 km/h से जाए तो 5 min early पहुँचता है। office की दूरी क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Multiplied the slow speed 5 km/h by the total late-early offset (15 min = 0.25 h) × 4 = 5. Distance requires subtracting two travel times d/5 − d/6 = 0.25.
options.trap_reason_hi: Slow speed 5 × 0.25 × 4 = 5 लगाया। सही: d/5 − d/6 = 0.25 से d निकलता है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Averaged the two speeds: (5 + 6)/2 = 5.5, and reported it as distance (wrong units — km/h is not km). The d-equation is d(1/5 − 1/6) = 0.25.
options.trap_reason_hi: दोनों speeds का average: (5 + 6)/2 = 5.5 को distance दे दिया (unit गलत — km/h km नहीं है)।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used gap in hours incorrectly: wrote time offset as 15 h instead of 15 min (skipped /60), then 5 × 2 = 10. Must convert minutes to hours (0.25 h).
options.trap_reason_hi: Time offset को 15 min की जगह 15 h मान लिया (/60 भूल गए), फिर 5 × 2 = 10। 15 min = 0.25 h चाहिए।

questions.explanation_method_en: Let distance = d km. Time at 5 km/h = d/5; at 6 km/h = d/6. The time difference is (10 + 5) = 15 min = 0.25 h (late to early). So d/5 − d/6 = 0.25 ⇒ d/30 = 0.25 ⇒ d = 7.5 km.
questions.explanation_concept_en: When two speeds give different arrival statuses (late vs early), both refer to the same 'on-time' clock. Time at the slow speed minus time at the fast speed equals total 'late + early' offset. That gap is the difference of two travel times for the same distance.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Distance = d km मानें। Time @ 5 km/h = d/5; @ 6 km/h = d/6। Time difference = 10 + 5 = 15 min = 0.25 h। d/5 − d/6 = 0.25 ⇒ d/30 = 0.25 ⇒ d = 7.5 km।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: दो speeds से अलग arrival statuses (late vs early) आएँ, तो दोनों 'on-time' clock से नापे जाते हैं। Slow speed पर time − fast speed पर time = कुल 'late + early' gap — same distance के दो travel times का अंतर।

questions.stem_en: A train passes a 200 m long platform in 25 seconds and a 150 m long platform in 20 seconds. Find the length of the train.
questions.stem_hi: एक train 200 m के platform को 25 sec में और 150 m के platform को 20 sec में cross करती है। Train की length क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Averaged the two platform lengths: (200 + 150)/2 = 175, then subtracted a plausible-looking 75 to get 100. Not derived from the two-equation system.
options.trap_reason_hi: Platform lengths का average: (200 + 150)/2 = 175, फिर कोई 75 घटाकर 100 कर दिया। Two-equation system से निकालना था।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Took the total distance of the first crossing (train+platform = 200 + train_length) and assigned it as train length: mistook the sum for the train length alone.
options.trap_reason_hi: पहली crossing की total distance (200 + train length) को train length मान लिया।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracted the two platform lengths and halved: (200 − 150)/2 = 25, rough-scaled to 75. The equations give exactly 50, not this midway guess.
options.trap_reason_hi: Platform lengths को घटाकर आधा: (200 − 150)/2 = 25, फिर बढ़ाकर 75 कर दिया। Equations से सटीक 50 निकलता है।

questions.explanation_method_en: Let train length = L, speed = v (m/s). (L + 200) = 25v and (L + 150) = 20v. Subtract: 50 = 5v ⇒ v = 10 m/s. Then L + 200 = 250 ⇒ L = 50 m.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Crossing a platform, the train must cover its own length PLUS the platform length. Two separate crossings with the same train (and the same constant speed) give two linear equations in (L, v); subtracting eliminates L to expose v, then back-substitute for L. Always write the sum (L + platform) as the covered distance — missing the train's own length is the classic slip.
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Platform cross करते समय train को खुद की length PLUS platform length तय करनी होती है। एक ही train की दो crossings से दो linear equations (L, v) मिलते हैं — घटाकर L eliminate करें, v निकालें, फिर L। 'खुद की length भूलना' classic गलती है।

questions.stem_en: A and B start from the same point in the same direction. A walks at 4 km/h and B at 6 km/h. After how many hours will B be 5 km ahead of A?
questions.stem_hi: A और B एक ही जगह से एक ही दिशा में निकलते हैं। A की speed 4 km/h, B की 6 km/h। कितने घंटों बाद B, A से 5 km आगे होगा?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Divided gap 5 by only A's speed 4: 5/4 = 1.25. Used one train's speed instead of the relative speed (6 − 4 = 2).
options.trap_reason_hi: Gap 5 को केवल A की speed 4 से भाग दिया: 5/4 = 1.25। Relative speed (6 − 4 = 2) लगानी थी।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Added the speeds: 4 + 6 = 10; then 5/10 = 0.5, doubled for 'return trip' assumption = 1. Relative speed for same direction SUBTRACTS.
options.trap_reason_hi: Speeds जोड़े (4 + 6 = 10): 5/10 = 0.5, × 2 = 1 किया। Same direction में घटाते हैं।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used gap 5 over SUM of speeds 10 (opposite-direction formula): 5/10 = 0.5. Same direction requires DIFFERENCE (2 km/h).
options.trap_reason_hi: Gap 5 को SUM 10 से भाग दिया (opposite-direction formula): 0.5। Same direction में DIFFERENCE (2 km/h) लगती है।

questions.explanation_method_en: Relative speed (same direction) = 6 − 4 = 2 km/h. Time to open a 5 km gap = 5/2 = 2.5 hours.
questions.explanation_concept_en: When two objects move in the same direction, the faster one opens up a gap at a rate equal to the DIFFERENCE of their speeds. In opposite directions the rate is the SUM. Always ask which frame of reference the problem wants (catch-up vs meet vs overtake).
questions.explanation_method_hi: Relative speed (same direction) = 6 − 4 = 2 km/h। 5 km का gap खुलने में time = 5/2 = 2.5 घंटे।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Same direction में तेज़ वाली object gap को speeds के अंतर की दर से खोलती है। विपरीत direction में यह दर sum होती है। Catch-up/meet/overtake — पहले तय करें।

questions.stem_en: A man rows 20 km downstream in 2 hours and the same distance upstream in 5 hours. Find the speed of the stream.
questions.stem_hi: एक व्यक्ति 20 km downstream 2 घंटे में और same distance upstream 5 घंटे में तय करता है। Stream की speed क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Reported still-water speed (10 + 4)/2 = 7 instead of stream speed. Still-water = boat; stream = half the DIFFERENCE.
options.trap_reason_hi: Still-water speed (10 + 4)/2 = 7 दे दी, stream नहीं। Boat = आधा SUM; stream = आधा DIFFERENCE।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used full difference (10 − 4 = 6) without halving. Stream speed = HALF the difference of downstream and upstream.
options.trap_reason_hi: Full difference (10 − 4 = 6) दे दी, आधा नहीं किया। Stream = आधा DIFFERENCE।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used upstream/2 = 4/2 = 2; interpreted 'stream' as half of upstream speed. Correct is half of (downstream − upstream).
options.trap_reason_hi: Upstream/2 = 4/2 = 2 लगा दिया (थोड़ा adjust करके 2.5)। सही: आधा (downstream − upstream) = (10 − 4)/2 = 3।

questions.explanation_method_en: Downstream = 20/2 = 10 km/h. Upstream = 20/5 = 4 km/h. Stream = (10 − 4)/2 = 3 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Downstream − Upstream = 2 × stream (the boat speed cancels), so stream speed = half the difference. Similarly (downstream + upstream)/2 gives the boat's still-water speed. Memorize the 'half-sum, half-difference' pair — exam questions will ask for one or the other.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Downstream = 20/2 = 10 km/h। Upstream = 20/5 = 4 km/h। Stream = (10 − 4)/2 = 3 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Downstream − Upstream = 2 × stream (boat speed cancel), अतः stream = आधा difference। Still-water boat = आधा sum। 'आधा-sum, आधा-difference' pair याद रखें।

questions.stem_en: A train crosses a man walking in the same direction at 6 km/h in 10 seconds. If the speed of the train is 60 km/h, find the length of the train.
questions.stem_hi: एक train same direction में 6 km/h से चलते आदमी को 10 sec में cross करती है। Train की speed 60 km/h हो तो Train की length क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used train speed 60 km/h alone (ignored the walker's 6 km/h): 60 × 5/18 × 10 = 600/18 × 10 = 166.67 m. Same direction requires relative speed 60 − 6.
options.trap_reason_hi: Train की 60 km/h अकेली लगाई (walker की 6 km/h छोड़ दी): 60 × 5/18 × 10 = 166.67 m। Same direction में relative 60 − 6 लगेगी।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used walker's speed 6 km/h as relative (read the question as 'crosses walker at 6 km/h relative'): 6 × 5/18 × 10 × 6 = 100. Arithmetic slip compounded with reading error.
options.trap_reason_hi: Walker की 6 km/h को relative मान लिया: 6 × 5/18 × 10 × 6 = 100। Reading error के साथ arithmetic slip।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Added speeds instead of subtracting: 60 + 6 = 66 km/h → 66 × 5/18 × 10 = 183.33 m. Addition is for opposite direction; same direction requires subtraction.
options.trap_reason_hi: Speeds जोड़े (60 + 6 = 66 km/h): 66 × 5/18 × 10 = 183.33 m। जोड़ना opposite direction के लिए है, same के लिए घटाते हैं।

questions.explanation_method_en: Relative speed (same direction) = 60 − 6 = 54 km/h = 54 × 5/18 = 15 m/s. Length = 15 × 10 = 150 m.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Crossing a moving observer is framed in the observer's frame of reference: the effective speed is the RELATIVE speed. For same direction subtract, for opposite add. The crossing distance equals the train's length because the observer is a point.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Relative speed (same direction) = 60 − 6 = 54 km/h = 54 × 5/18 = 15 m/s। Length = 15 × 10 = 150 m।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: चलते observer को cross करना observer के frame में देखें: effective speed = RELATIVE speed। Same direction में घटाएँ, विपरीत में जोड़ें। Crossing distance = train की length (observer point है)।

questions.stem_en: A runner can complete a 1 km race in 4 minutes. How many metres does he run in 12 seconds?
questions.stem_hi: एक runner 1 km दौड़ 4 मिनट में पूरी करता है। 12 sec में वह कितने metre दौड़ता है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used 1 km / 4 min = 0.25 km/min = 250 m/min → 250/5 = 50, but split into 48 via rounding 4 min = 240 s error pathway.
options.trap_reason_hi: 1 km/4 min = 250 m/min; 12 s/60 = 0.2 min; 250 × 0.2 = 50, पर ratio चक्कर में 48 निकाल लिया।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Divided distance 1 by time 4: 1/4 = 0.25, then scaled by 12 = 3. Did not convert km to metres before scaling.
options.trap_reason_hi: Distance 1 को time 4 से भाग दिया: 0.25 × 12 = 3। km को metre में बदलना भूल गए।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used 4 min = 4 × 60 s = 240 s, ratio 1000 m / 240 s = 25/6 m/s, then 25/6 × 24 = 100. Used 24 s instead of 12 s.

questions.explanation_method_en: Speed = 1000 m / 240 s = 25/6 m/s. Distance in 12 s = 25/6 × 12 = 50 m.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Always normalize to a common unit system before scaling. Here: convert 1 km to 1000 m and 4 min to 240 s to get speed in m/s, then multiply by the target 12 s. Staying in mixed units (km and minutes) almost always leads to a factor-60 or factor-1000 slip.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Speed = 1000 m / 240 s = 25/6 m/s। 12 sec में distance = 25/6 × 12 = 50 m।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Scaling से पहले units को एकसमान करें। यहाँ 1 km = 1000 m, 4 min = 240 s से speed m/s में, फिर × 12 sec। Mixed units में रहने पर 60 या 1000 का factor slip होता है।

questions.stem_en: Two trains leave stations A and B simultaneously towards each other at 50 km/h and 70 km/h. They meet after 3 hours. What is the distance between A and B?
questions.stem_hi: दो trains stations A और B से एक-दूसरे की ओर एक साथ 50 km/h और 70 km/h से चलती हैं। 3 घंटे में मिलती हैं। A और B के बीच की दूरी क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracted the speeds (70 − 50 = 20 km/h) and multiplied by 3: 20 × 3 = 60. Opposite-direction relative speed is the SUM, not difference.
options.trap_reason_hi: Speeds घटाए (70 − 50 = 20) × 3 = 60। विपरीत दिशा में relative = SUM।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used one train's speed only: 60 × 3 = 180 (averaged 50 and 70 then × 3). The correct formula uses the combined 120 km/h closing speed.
options.trap_reason_hi: एक ही train की speed लगाई: 60 × 3 = 180 (50 और 70 का average × 3)। सही 120 km/h closing speed × 3।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Reported the closing speed (50 + 70 = 120 km/h) as the distance — forgot to multiply by the 3 hours.
options.trap_reason_hi: Closing speed (50 + 70 = 120 km/h) को distance मान लिया — × 3 घंटे नहीं किया।

questions.explanation_method_en: Closing speed = 50 + 70 = 120 km/h. Distance = closing speed × time = 120 × 3 = 360 km.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Two objects moving toward each other close their gap at a rate equal to the SUM of their speeds. The initial gap is closed in time × sum, which equals the distance between their starting points. Symmetric idea to same-direction problems where the closing rate is the DIFFERENCE.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Closing speed = 50 + 70 = 120 km/h। Distance = closing × time = 120 × 3 = 360 km।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: दो objects एक-दूसरे की ओर आ रहे हों तो gap speeds के SUM की दर से बंद होता है। Initial gap = time × sum = starting points के बीच की distance। Same-direction में closing rate = DIFFERENCE।

questions.stem_en: A car driver covers a distance in 4 hours at 60 km/h. If he increases his speed by 20 km/h, how long will the same journey take?
questions.stem_hi: एक car driver 60 km/h से 4 घंटे में एक दूरी तय करता है। अगर वह speed 20 km/h बढ़ा दे, तो वही यात्रा कितने समय में पूरी होगी?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracted 0.5 h from 4 h treating the 20 km/h jump as roughly a 1/8 speed increase: 4 − 0.5 = 3.5. Actual ratio 60/80 = 3/4 gives new time = 3 h.
options.trap_reason_hi: 4 − 0.5 = 3.5 किया (20 km/h jump को ~1/8 बढ़त मानकर)। सही ratio 60/80 = 3/4 ⇒ 3 घंटे।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Applied additive subtraction: 20/60 × 4 = 1.33 h reduction → 4 − 1.33 = 2.67. Time scaling is MULTIPLICATIVE (× 3/4 = 3), not additive.
options.trap_reason_hi: Additive subtraction: 20/60 × 4 = 1.33 घटाकर 4 − 1.33 = 2.67 किया। Time scaling multiplicative है (× 3/4 = 3), additive नहीं।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Multiplied 4 × 1.2 = 4.8 (treated 20 km/h increase as 20% of time added). Distance is constant — speed up ⇒ time DOWN, not up.
options.trap_reason_hi: 4 × 1.2 = 4.8 किया (20 km/h बढ़त को 20% time increase मान लिया)। Distance constant ⇒ speed ↑ → time ↓।

questions.explanation_method_en: Distance = 60 × 4 = 240 km. New speed = 80 km/h. New time = 240 / 80 = 3 h. Shortcut: time ∝ 1/speed for fixed distance, so new_time/4 = 60/80 = 3/4, new_time = 3 h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: For a fixed distance, time and speed are inversely proportional. Doubling speed halves time; a 1/3 speed increase drops time by 1/4 (because 3/4 × 4 = 3). Never subtract speed absolutely and expect a linear time change — use the ratio.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Distance = 60 × 4 = 240 km। नई speed = 80 km/h। नया time = 240/80 = 3 h। Shortcut: fixed distance पर time ∝ 1/speed, new_time/4 = 60/80 = 3/4, new_time = 3 h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Fixed distance पर time और speed inversely proportional हैं। Double speed → half time; 1/3 बढ़ी speed → time 1/4 कम (3/4 × 4 = 3)। Speed को absolute घटाकर linear time change की अपेक्षा न करें — ratio लगाएँ।

questions.stem_en: A man travels from X to Y at 30 km/h and returns at 40 km/h. If the total journey took 7 hours, find the distance between X and Y.
questions.stem_hi: एक आदमी X से Y तक 30 km/h और वापस 40 km/h से लौटता है। कुल यात्रा 7 घंटे चली। X और Y के बीच की दूरी क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used arithmetic-mean speed (30 + 40)/2 = 35 km/h × (7/2) = 122.5 km for the one-way leg. Equal-distance legs need the HARMONIC mean for average speed, not arithmetic.
options.trap_reason_hi: Arithmetic mean (30 + 40)/2 = 35 × (7/2) = 122.5 km एक leg के लिए। Equal-distance में HARMONIC mean चाहिए।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Reported the ROUND-TRIP total distance (2 × 120 = 240) as the one-way distance. Question asks for one-way X to Y.
options.trap_reason_hi: Round-trip total (2 × 120 = 240) को one-way मान लिया। Question one-way X से Y पूछ रहा है।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Added speeds 30 + 40 = 70 and dropped the time factor entirely. Dimensions break — km/h alone is not km.
options.trap_reason_hi: Speeds जोड़े (30 + 40 = 70), time factor छोड़ दिया। Dimensions गलत — km/h अकेले km नहीं है।

questions.explanation_method_en: Let distance = d. Time going = d/30, returning = d/40. Total: d/30 + d/40 = 7 ⇒ d × (4 + 3)/120 = 7 ⇒ 7d/120 = 7 ⇒ d = 120 km.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Same distance in both directions but different speeds ⇒ add the two travel times (not average them). The equation d/v₁ + d/v₂ = T collapses to d × (v₁ + v₂)/(v₁ × v₂) = T, i.e., d = T × v₁ × v₂ / (v₁ + v₂). That's T × half the harmonic mean.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Distance = d। जाते time = d/30, वापस = d/40। Total: d/30 + d/40 = 7 ⇒ d × 7/120 = 7 ⇒ d = 120 km।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: दोनों दिशाओं में same distance पर अलग speeds ⇒ travel times जोड़ें (average नहीं)। Equation d/v₁ + d/v₂ = T से d = T × v₁ × v₂ /(v₁ + v₂) — T × आधा harmonic mean।

questions.stem_en: A train running at 45 km/h crosses a bridge in 30 seconds. If the length of the bridge is 250 m, find the length of the train.
questions.stem_hi: एक train 45 km/h से चलते हुए एक bridge को 30 sec में cross करती है। Bridge की length 250 m हो तो Train की length क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Reported total distance covered (= train length + bridge length = 375) as the train length alone. Forgot to subtract the bridge 250.
options.trap_reason_hi: Total covered distance (= train length + bridge = 375) को train length मान लिया। Bridge 250 घटाना भूल गए।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Assumed train length equals bridge length (250). They are independent — train length comes from total distance − bridge.
options.trap_reason_hi: Train length को bridge length (250) के बराबर मान लिया। ये independent हैं।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used memorized speed 54 km/h = 15 m/s instead of converting 45 km/h: 15 × 30 = 450 total, 450 − 250 = 200. Speed 45 km/h = 12.5 m/s gives 375 total, 125 train.

questions.explanation_method_en: Speed in m/s = 45 × 5/18 = 12.5 m/s. Total distance covered = 12.5 × 30 = 375 m. Length of train = total − bridge = 375 − 250 = 125 m.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Crossing a bridge, the covered distance is train's length + bridge's length. Given time and speed, compute total distance, then subtract the bridge length to isolate the train's length.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Speed m/s में = 45 × 5/18 = 12.5 m/s। Total covered = 12.5 × 30 = 375 m। Train length = total − bridge = 375 − 250 = 125 m।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Bridge cross करते समय covered distance = train length + bridge length। Time और speed से total distance निकालकर bridge length घटाएँ।

questions.stem_en: Two pipes fill a tank in 12 hours and 18 hours respectively. If both are opened together, how long will they take to fill the tank?
questions.stem_hi: दो pipes एक tank को क्रमशः 12 घंटे और 18 घंटे में भरती हैं। दोनों एक साथ खोलने पर tank कितने समय में भरेगा?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Averaged the two times (12 + 18)/2 = 15. Pipe rates add when they work in parallel; the resulting time is always LESS than either individual time.
options.trap_reason_hi: Times का average (12 + 18)/2 = 15 निकाल दिया। Parallel pipes की rates जुड़ती हैं — resulting time हर एक से कम होता है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Added the two times (12 + 18 = 30). Additive time is only for sequential work — for parallel, rates add not times.
options.trap_reason_hi: Times जोड़ दिए (12 + 18 = 30)। Sequential में time जुड़ता है, parallel में rate जुड़ती है।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Half of the smaller time (12/2 = 6). The combined rate formula is 1/T = 1/12 + 1/18 giving T = 36/5 = 7.2, not 6.

questions.explanation_method_en: Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 3/36 + 2/36 = 5/36 tanks per hour. Time = 36/5 = 7.2 hours.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Rate problems parallel speed problems one-for-one: if pipe A fills the tank in a hours and B in b hours, their combined rate is 1/a + 1/b (tanks per hour), and the combined time is ab/(a+b). The formula is the harmonic mean's close cousin — same structure as 'equal distance, two speeds' average speed.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Combined rate = 1/12 + 1/18 = 5/36 tanks/hour। Time = 36/5 = 7.2 घंटे।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Rate problems speed problems की तरह: pipe A तt a घंटे में, B b घंटे में भरे तो combined rate = 1/a + 1/b, combined time = ab/(a+b)। 'Equal distance, two speeds' average speed जैसा structure।

questions.stem_en: A boat's downstream speed is 15 km/h and the speed of the stream is 3 km/h. Find the time taken to travel 36 km upstream.
questions.stem_hi: एक boat की downstream speed 15 km/h है और stream की speed 3 km/h है। Upstream 36 km जाने में कितना time लगेगा?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used downstream speed 15 km/h: 36/15 = 2.4. Question asks UPSTREAM (which is 9 km/h).
options.trap_reason_hi: Downstream speed 15 km/h लगाई: 36/15 = 2.4। Question UPSTREAM (9 km/h) पूछ रहा है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used stream speed 3 km/h: 36/3 = 12. Stream alone doesn't move the boat — upstream speed = boat − stream = 12 − 3 = 9, not 3.
options.trap_reason_hi: Stream speed 3 km/h लगाई: 36/3 = 12। Stream अकेले boat को नहीं चलाती — upstream = boat − stream = 12 − 3 = 9।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Added stream speed to downstream (15 + 3 = 18) → 36/18 = 2, then adjusted to 3. Upstream = downstream − 2 × stream = 15 − 6 = 9; time = 36/9 = 4.
options.trap_reason_hi: Downstream में stream जोड़ा (15 + 3 = 18) → 36/18 = 2, फिर 3 कर दिया। सही Upstream = downstream − 2 × stream = 9, time = 4 घंटे।

questions.explanation_method_en: Boat speed in still water = downstream − stream = 15 − 3 = 12 km/h. Upstream speed = 12 − 3 = 9 km/h. Time = 36/9 = 4 hours.
questions.explanation_concept_en: To find the upstream speed when only downstream and stream are given: first recover the boat's still-water speed (= downstream − stream), then upstream = still-water − stream. Equivalently, upstream = downstream − 2 × stream (because the stream aids downstream and opposes upstream, so it switches sign twice).
questions.explanation_method_hi: Boat still-water speed = downstream − stream = 15 − 3 = 12 km/h। Upstream = 12 − 3 = 9 km/h। Time = 36/9 = 4 घंटे।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Downstream और stream दी हों, तो पहले boat still-water (= downstream − stream), फिर upstream = still-water − stream। समकक्ष: upstream = downstream − 2 × stream।

questions.stem_en: A train running at 'x' km/h crosses a platform 300 m long in 24 seconds, and a pole in 9 seconds. Find the speed of the train in km/h.
questions.stem_hi: एक train 'x' km/h पर 300 m के platform को 24 sec में और एक pole को 9 sec में cross करती है। Train की speed km/h में ज्ञात कीजिए।

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used platform 300 m / 24 s = 12.5 m/s = 45 km/h, treating the crossing distance as just the platform. The crossing also needs the train's own length.
options.trap_reason_hi: Platform 300 m / 24 s = 12.5 m/s = 45 km/h लगा दिया — crossing distance में train की length भूल गए।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used pole time × large speed guess: 300 m / 9 s = 33.33 m/s = 120 km/h. Pole crossing gives train-length/9 = speed, not platform/9.
options.trap_reason_hi: 300/9 = 33.33 m/s = 120 km/h लगाया। Pole crossing train-length/9 = speed देता है, 300/9 नहीं।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used 300 m / (24 − 9) = 20 m/s = 72 km/h and bumped to 80 by adding a fictitious 'slack'. The correct value is exactly 72, not 80.
options.trap_reason_hi: 300/(24−9) = 20 m/s = 72 km/h निकाला पर 'slack' जोड़कर 80 कर दिया। सही value 72 है।

questions.explanation_method_en: Let train length = L, speed = v (m/s). Pole: L = 9v. Platform: L + 300 = 24v. Subtract: 300 = 15v ⇒ v = 20 m/s = 20 × 18/5 = 72 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: The two crossings share the same train length L and same speed v. Subtracting the pole equation from the platform equation eliminates L, isolating v from the 300 m difference in covered distance and the 15 s difference in time. This 'eliminate by subtraction' pattern is the workhorse of two-crossing problems.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Train length = L, speed = v (m/s) मानें। Pole: L = 9v। Platform: L + 300 = 24v। घटाएँ: 300 = 15v ⇒ v = 20 m/s = 72 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: दोनों crossings में same L और v। Platform-pole घटाने से L cancel, 300 m extra और 15 s extra से v निकल आती है। यह 'घटाकर eliminate' pattern two-crossing problems का basic trick है।

questions.stem_en: A man can row 9 km/h in still water. He takes twice as long to row upstream as downstream. Find the speed of the stream.
questions.stem_hi: एक व्यक्ति still water में 9 km/h row कर सकता है। वह upstream में downstream से दोगुना समय लेता है। Stream की speed क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Took half of boat speed: 9/2 = 4.5. The '2:1 time ratio' condition sets downstream/upstream = 2, which translates to (9+s)/(9−s) = 2 ⇒ s = 3.
options.trap_reason_hi: Boat speed का आधा: 9/2 = 4.5 दे दी। Condition (9+s)/(9−s) = 2 से s = 3।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Took 1/6 of boat speed: 9/6 = 1.5. Set the ratio equation wrong — used downstream/upstream = 3 instead of 2.
options.trap_reason_hi: Boat का 1/6: 9/6 = 1.5 दे दी। Ratio 2 की जगह 3 लगाया — (9+s)/(9−s) = 3 से s = 4.5... not 1.5 either. सही 2 से s = 3।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used 2/3 of boat speed: 9 × 2/3 = 6. Stream can't equal two-thirds of boat speed while preserving a positive upstream — upstream would be 9 − 6 = 3, still valid arithmetically, but the ratio check fails: 15/3 = 5 ≠ 2.
options.trap_reason_hi: Boat का 2/3: 9 × 2/3 = 6। यहाँ upstream = 3, downstream = 15, ratio = 15/3 = 5 ≠ 2 — condition fail।

questions.explanation_method_en: Let stream = s. Downstream speed = 9 + s; upstream = 9 − s. Time ratio upstream:downstream = 2:1 ⇒ (9 + s)/(9 − s) = 2 ⇒ 9 + s = 18 − 2s ⇒ 3s = 9 ⇒ s = 3 km/h.
questions.explanation_concept_en: For the same DISTANCE, time ratio equals the inverse of speed ratio. 'Upstream takes twice as long as downstream' ⇒ downstream speed is twice upstream speed ⇒ (9+s) = 2(9−s). One linear equation in s.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Stream = s। Downstream = 9 + s, upstream = 9 − s। Same distance पर time ratio upstream:downstream = 2:1 ⇒ speeds ratio downstream:upstream = 2:1 ⇒ (9+s)/(9−s) = 2 ⇒ s = 3 km/h।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Same distance पर time ratio = speeds का inverse। 'Upstream में दोगुना समय' ⇒ downstream speed upstream की दोगुनी ⇒ एक linear equation।

questions.stem_en: Two cars start from the same point at the same time. Car A runs at 60 km/h and Car B at 75 km/h. After 4 hours they are not on the same road — Car B's path makes a 90° angle with Car A's. How far apart are they?
questions.stem_hi: दो cars एक ही point से एक साथ शुरू होती हैं। Car A 60 km/h पर, Car B 75 km/h पर। 4 घंटे बाद — वे अलग-अलग roads पर हैं, जहाँ Car B का रास्ता Car A के रास्ते से 90° का कोण बनाता है। अब वे कितनी दूर होंगी?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Summed the two distances directly: 60×4 + 75×4 = 240 + 300 = 540. That's the path length along the roads, not the straight-line separation at 90°.
options.trap_reason_hi: दोनों distances जोड़ दीं: 240 + 300 = 540। यह roads पर chलने की दूरी है, 90° पर separation नहीं।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Subtracted the two distances: 300 − 240 = 60. Subtraction is for same-direction tracks — these are perpendicular, so use Pythagoras.
options.trap_reason_hi: Distances घटाईं: 300 − 240 = 60। घटाना same-direction के लिए — ये perpendicular हैं, Pythagoras लगेगा।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Averaged the two distances: (240 + 300)/2 = 270. Averaging has no geometric meaning for a right-angle separation.
options.trap_reason_hi: दोनों distances का average: (240 + 300)/2 = 270 — यह ज्यामितीय रूप से कोई meaning नहीं रखता।

questions.explanation_method_en: A travels 60 × 4 = 240 km; B travels 75 × 4 = 300 km. Paths are perpendicular, so separation = √(240² + 300²) = √(57,600 + 90,000) = √147,600 ≈ 384.18 km. (Accept 384 or 384.09 depending on rounding.)
questions.explanation_concept_en: When two paths start from the same point at right angles, the separation is the hypotenuse: √(a² + b²). This is straight-line Pythagoras applied to the two leg distances. Path-sum and path-difference shortcuts do NOT apply for non-parallel tracks.
questions.explanation_concept_hi: एक ही point से 90° पर शुरू हुए रास्तों की separation hypotenuse होती है: √(a² + b²)। Non-parallel tracks में path-sum या path-difference shortcuts नहीं चलते।

questions.stem_en: The ratio of speeds of two trains is 5:7. If the second train takes 3 hours less than the first to cover the same distance, and the first train's speed is 50 km/h, find the distance.
questions.stem_hi: दो trains की speeds का ratio 5:7 है। दूसरी train same distance पर पहली से 3 घंटे कम लेती है। पहली की speed 50 km/h हो तो distance क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used 50 × 3 = 150 treating the 3-hour gap as the first train's total travel time. The 3-hour gap is the DIFFERENCE of times, not either train's full time.
options.trap_reason_hi: 50 × 3 = 150 को first train का total time मान लिया। 3-hour gap दोनों times का अंतर है।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used second train's speed (70 km/h) × the gap (3 hr) × adjustment: 70 × 5 = 350. Muddled which speed/time pair should multiply.
options.trap_reason_hi: Second train की speed (70) × 5 = 350 लगाया — गलत pair multiply हुआ।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used the ratio-induced time pair wrong: took first train's time = 7 h, distance = 50 × 14 = 700 (used the ratio labels 5:7 as times in hours).
options.trap_reason_hi: Times को ratio labels (5, 7) से सीधे hours मान लिया: 50 × 14 = 700।

questions.explanation_method_en: Speeds 5:7 ⇒ second speed = 50 × 7/5 = 70 km/h. Times are inversely proportional: time ratio 7:5. Let times be 7k and 5k; difference 2k = 3 ⇒ k = 1.5. First train time = 7 × 1.5 = 10.5 h. Distance = 50 × 10.5 = 525 km. Verify with second: 70 × 7.5 = 525 ✓.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Speed ratio ↔ inverse time ratio (for the same distance). Parameterize both times by a common factor k, let the difference equate to the given gap, solve k, then compute the distance with either speed and its matching time.
questions.explanation_method_hi: Speeds 5:7 ⇒ second speed = 50 × 7/5 = 70 km/h। Times का inverse ratio 7:5। Times = 7k, 5k; difference 2k = 3 ⇒ k = 1.5। First time = 7 × 1.5 = 10.5 h। Distance = 50 × 10.5 = 525 km। Verify: 70 × 7.5 = 525 ✓।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: Same distance पर speed ratio और time ratio inverse होते हैं। दोनों times को common factor k से parameterize करें, difference से k निकालें, फिर distance = speed × matching time।

questions.stem_en: A train overtakes two persons walking at 2 km/h and 4 km/h in the same direction in 9 seconds and 10 seconds respectively. Find the length of the train.
questions.stem_hi: एक train same direction में चलते दो व्यक्तियों (क्रमशः 2 km/h और 4 km/h से) को 9 sec और 10 sec में overtake करती है। Train की length क्या है?

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Used v = 55/9 m/s ≈ 6.11 directly as the length (confused the intermediate v-value with L): 55/9 × 9 seems plausible but the correct L = 9v − 5 = 9 × 55/9 − 5 = 50.
options.trap_reason_hi: Intermediate v = 55/9 m/s को ही length समझ लिया: 55/9 × 9 ≈ 55। सही: L = 9v − 5 = 50।

options.label: C
options.trap_reason_en: Used only the 10-second equation with walker speed 4 km/h: L = 10(v − 10/9) with an incorrect v of 20 km/h = 50/9 m/s → L = 10 × (50/9 − 10/9) = 400/9 = 44.44. Wrong v value.
options.trap_reason_hi: केवल 10-sec equation लगाई: L = 10(v − 10/9) में गलत v = 50/9 m/s → 400/9 = 44.44। सही v = 55/9 m/s।

options.label: D
options.trap_reason_en: Used only the 9-second equation with walker 2 km/h: L = 9(v − 5/9) with v = 22.22 km/h ≈ 55/9 × 1.04 m/s giving ~57.22 m. Forgot the constraint from the second equation that pins v exactly to 55/9.
options.trap_reason_hi: केवल 9-sec equation (walker 2 km/h) लगाई, दूसरे equation से v को pin नहीं किया: गलत v से 57.22 m मिला। सही v = 55/9 m/s।

questions.explanation_method_en: Let train speed = v (m/s), length = L. Walker speeds in m/s = 5/9 and 10/9. Equations: L = 9(v − 5/9) and L = 10(v − 10/9). Equate: 9v − 5 = 10v − 100/9 ⇒ v = 100/9 − 45/9 = 55/9 m/s (≈ 22 km/h). Then L = 9 × 55/9 − 5 = 55 − 5 = 50 m. Verify with the 10-s equation: 10 × (55/9 − 10/9) = 10 × 45/9 = 50 ✓.
questions.explanation_concept_en: Two overtakings of walkers at different speeds give two linear equations in L and v. Subtracting eliminates L, exposing v in terms of the time gap (1 s) and the relative-speed gap (5/9 m/s). Back-substitute v to get L. The walker's speed subtracts from the train's (same-direction relative speed).
questions.explanation_method_hi: Train speed = v (m/s), length = L। Walker speeds m/s में 5/9 और 10/9। Equations: L = 9(v − 5/9), L = 10(v − 10/9)। Equate: 9v − 5 = 10v − 100/9 ⇒ v = 55/9 m/s (≈ 22 km/h)। L = 9 × 55/9 − 5 = 50 m। Verify: 10 × (55/9 − 10/9) = 10 × 45/9 = 50 ✓।
questions.explanation_concept_hi: दो walkers (अलग speeds) पर overtaking से L और v के दो linear equations। घटाने से L eliminate होकर v मिल जाता है (time gap 1 s और relative-speed gap 5/9 m/s से)। Same direction में walker की speed train से घटती है।

questions.stem_en: Find the LCM of 18 and 24.
questions.stem_hi: 18 और 24 का LCM ज्ञात कीजिए।

options.label: B
options.trap_reason_en: Reported HCF 6 instead of LCM. LCM is the least common multiple, not the greatest common factor.
options.trap_reason_hi: HCF 6 को LCM मान लिया। LCM least common multiple होता है, greatest factor नहीं।

options.label: C
